ctx0003: supercomputer for the scientist s work tone: professional include research questions The second step is 2. Identify Strategic Hedges and Options In an uncertain world flexibility has tremendous
ctx0004: value. If at the of your commute you foresee the potential for traffic congestion for instance you might choose to avoid an elevated highway with few off-ramps or
ctx0005: on-ramps and take a more flexible if longer route to save time. Likewise leaders can incorporate the value of flexibility in the form of hedges and options into
ctx0006: strategic decision-making. Real options analysis emerged as a methodology to help companies better account for this value. A real option represents a right but not an obligation to
ctx0007: undertake some business decision means that the option holder can delay further investment until additional information has been analyzed. Techniques based on financial option pricing allow analysts the
ctx0008: right data to quantify the value of being able to delay or walk away from an investment. In the early 2000s real options analysis was widely seen as
ctx0009: a cutting-edge tool for valuing natural resource investments formulating new product-development plans analyzing R D spending making acquisitions and evaluating technology investments. Sadly it has never really taken
ctx0010: hold in strategic planning. Strategists have struggled to specify the model s parameters sufficiently to render its output useful to them. In our view companies have sidelined real
ctx0011: options analysis prematurely paying far too much attention to the mechanics of determining the absolute value of a specific real option rather than relying on the tool s
ctx0012: basic principles to understand the relative value of competing strategy alternatives. For example if two alternatives are equally capable of addressing a company s competitive issues but one
ctx0013: provides greater flexibility to alter course defer investment or redeploy resources on the basis of better information that one has a higher relative value in most instances and
ctx0014: be selected. We encourage leaders to account for the value of flexibility in evaluating strategy alternatives even when it is challenging or maybe impossible to quantify it absolutely.
ctx0015: Strategic flexibility has a variety of sources. In some instances new technology can create it. For instance many railroads are replacing aging locomotives with new engines. As the
ctx0016: volatility of fuel prices has increased deciding fuel to choose for future locomotives has become critical. Starting in 2016 the spread between oil and natural gas prices increased
ctx0017: significantly creating an opportunity to shift to natural gas to fuel future locomotives. But no one be sure whether the spread persist or whether it widen or narrow.
ctx0018: At the same time locomotive technology was changing with flexible-fuel locomotives emerging as a viable alternative to single-fuel engines. The latter are less expensive but obviously far less
ctx0019: flexible. Depending on a railroad s route structure customer base and strategy the flexibility offered by the new engines can offset the additional up-front cost of purchasing them.
ctx0020: Often though the situation is less clear-cut and leadership teams choose between options with wildly differing payoffs depending on how the future unfolds. By accounting for the value
ctx0021: of flexibility executives can often uncover strategies that allow them to hedge bets while preserving valuable options for the future. Walt Disney is a case in point. Several
ctx0022: years ago Disney s leaders realized that streaming was threatening the viability of traditional cable and satellite services. In August 2017 Disney announced that it acquire majority ownership
ctx0023: of BAMTech a global leader in direct-to-consumer streaming technology and marketing services. Disney s 1.58 billion investment caused a stir at the time leaving investors wondering why the
ctx0024: company put so much into a relatively small platform an offshoot of Major League Baseball . But that acquisition put the company in a position to launch Disney+
ctx0025: just over two years later. Four months its investment in BAMTech Disney initiated an acquisition of 21st Century Fox. At first that 71.3 billion investment looked like an
ctx0026: attempt to double down on traditional broadcast media. But the acquisition was in fact a hedge providing Disney with access to Fox s extensive film library a collection
ctx0027: that be valuable no matter distribution model persevered over the long term. Disney s investments in strategic hedges and options enabled the company to pull far ahead of
ctx0028: less-prescient rivals and led to a doubling of its market value between 2017 and 2020. Other organizations are following Disney s example as part of long-term strategy. It
ctx0029: s important to note that investing in strategic hedges and options is not the same as placing a bet on every square. Trying to reduce risk in that
ctx0030: way makes it nearly impossible for a company to score a big win all we ve seen few examples of relatively small positions being parlayed into market leadership
ctx0031: and locks in the certainty of many small losses that add up to real money. Worse yet juggling a list of competing bets dilutes leadership focus wastes precious
ctx0032: time and squanders scarce talent further compounding the damage. Consider Dell once In the years before 2013 not knowing how technologies play out Dell invested across the board
ctx0033: in smartphones tablets Chromebooks printers consumer PCs commercial laptops and desktops workstations servers storage IT services and software. Beyond PCs workstations and servers Dell was a distant follower
ctx0034: in most of the markets in it had paid handsomely to play. Jamie Chung/Trunk Archive Dell anchored its decision-making in extreme but plausible scenarios ranging from the death
ctx0035: of the PC to mobility everywhere did the leadership team realize how many of its small bets were unlikely to pay off. Smartphones tablets and printers were among
ctx0036: the first to be cut. Later the company jettisoned IT services and software. But the analysis also highlighted areas where it double down on its investments and potentially
ctx0037: win in the long term. Accordingly Dell simplified its PC and server portfolios and reinvested a portion of the resulting savings to improve product design. moves and others
ctx0038: helped Dell win as the personal computer and server markets commoditized and consolidated. By exiting multiple markets to focus its bets in a strategic way Dell drove up

ctx0026: and Relational Traits in Online Social Networks Abstract: Users in Online Social Networks OSNs leave traces that reflect personality characteristics. The study of traces is important for several
ctx0027: fields such as social science psychology marketing and others. Despite a marked increase in research on personality prediction based on online behavior the focus has been heavily on
ctx0028: individual personality traits and by doing so largely neglects relational facets of personality. This study aims to address this gap by providing a prediction model for holistic personality
ctx0029: profiling in OSNs that includes socio-relational traits attachment orientations in combination with standard personality traits. Specifically we first designed a feature engineering methodology that extracts a wide range
ctx0030: of features accounting for behavior language and emotions from the OSN accounts of users. Subsequently we designed a machine learning model that predicts trait scores of users based
ctx0031: on the extracted features. The proposed model architecture is inspired by characteristics embedded in psychology i.e it utilizes interrelations among personality facets and leads to increased accuracy in
ctx0032: comparison with other state-of-the-art approaches. To demonstrate the usefulness of this approach we applied our model on two datasets namely regular OSN users and opinion leaders on social
ctx0033: media and contrast both samples psychological profiles. Our findings demonstrate that the two groups can be clearly separated by focusing on both Big Five personality traits and attachment
ctx0034: orientations. The presented research provides a promising avenue for future research on OSN user characterization and classification. Introduction: Online Social Networks OSNs offer a virtual space in people
ctx0035: connect and interact with others express themselves and receive information in a continuous digital reflection of the real offline world. In OSNs people typically showcase real self 40
ctx0036: and leave traces in online behavior reflect real-world personality 24 . traces expose a holistic image of oneself including both personal characteristics personality traits and characteristics that portray
ctx0037: behavior in relation to others relational traits . The term personality refers to characteristic combinations or patterns of behaviors cognitions and emotional reactions that evolve from biological and
ctx0038: environmental factors and form relatively consistent individual differences 13 . The Big Five BF or Five Factor model 29 is one of the most distinctive personality theories that
ctx0039: constitutes five main traits of human personality representing individual differences in cognition emotion and behavior: Openness to Experience Conscientiousness Extraversion Agreeableness and Neuroticism. On the other hand relational
ctx0040: traits have also been linked with consistencies in social behavior and interaction patterns with attachment theory 7 as the most emblematic theoretical framework in that respect 31 43
ctx0041: capturing how individuals experience close relationships to and interactions with others. Personality traits have been studied in the context of OSNs and the web overall as findings show
ctx0042: that they are strongly linked to OSN use 57 online friendships 60 and online reviews 52 . Moreover certain prediction models have been proposed 37 64 to extract
ctx0043: users psychological background from online behavioral residue and map it to personality characteristics. However relational traits such as attachment orientations AO have been overlooked in online environments even
ctx0044: though user activity in OSNs heavily relates to social behavior characteristics. This makes the study of a relational profile critical from an application point of view and provides
ctx0045: rich information individuals social profile. The present research aims to address this limitation in OSN research by studying and predicting both relational traits and personality traits of users.
ctx0046: The importance of relational facets of personality for explaining social interaction cannot be overstated. that online social media engagement resembles actual social interactions in many respects 15 30
ctx0047: the need to study how different personality facets are reflected in online expression is particularly compelling. Attachment orientations a key individual difference of relational orientation can be derived
ctx0048: on the basis of traces found in micro-blogs. Attachment orientations capture one s notion of the self in relation to others and interpersonal relationships with attachment theory being
ctx0049: one of the key personality theoretical frames to actual social behavior 31 . Considering both traditional personality Big Five traits and relational traits is important for 1 providing
ctx0050: holistic profiling of OSN users humans have an integrated profile in self and social aspects interrelate and affect each other and joint profiling can be essential for understanding
ctx0051: the overall human presence on OSNs 2 uncovering more traits of people s psychological and social world has been identified as a direction in OSN research currently focuses
ctx0052: on the personality traits that help to better analyze and predict online user behavior 66 e.g. with applications on customer segmentation 46 or digital advertisement environments 17 and
ctx0053: 3 shedding light on social interaction phenomena taking place in OSNs is of great socioeconomic importance e.g. community formation 32 decision making 42 or information diffusion 12 .

ctx0056: and exploits the inherent correlations in psychological traits to efficiently predict a complete image of OSN users psychological profile. The proposed approach is applied on the Twitter micro-blogging
ctx0057: service stands as a live dynamic and open OSN platform on people intensively interact and is largely driven by people s spontaneous reactions and emotions expressing themselves personality
ctx0058: facet and interacting with others relational facet at the same time. Specifically our contributions in detail are as follows: Data mining and feature engineering for psychology traces in

ctx0018: fields such as social science psychology marketing and others. Despite a marked increase in research on personality prediction based on online behavior the focus has been heavily on
ctx0019: individual personality traits and by doing so largely neglects relational facets of personality. This study aims to address this gap by providing a prediction model for holistic personality
ctx0020: profiling in OSNs that includes socio-relational traits attachment orientations in combination with standard personality traits. Specifically we first designed a feature engineering methodology that extracts a wide range
ctx0021: of features accounting for behavior language and emotions from the OSN accounts of users. Subsequently we designed a machine learning model that predicts trait scores of users based
ctx0022: on the extracted features. The proposed model architecture is inspired by characteristics embedded in psychology i.e it utilizes interrelations among personality facets and leads to increased accuracy in
ctx0023: comparison with other state-of-the-art approaches. To demonstrate the usefulness of this approach we applied our model on two datasets namely regular OSN users and opinion leaders on social
ctx0024: media and contrast both samples psychological profiles. Our findings demonstrate that the two groups can be clearly separated by focusing on both Big Five personality traits and attachment
ctx0025: orientations. The presented research provides a promising avenue for future research on OSN user characterization and classification. Introduction: Online Social Networks OSNs offer a virtual space in people
ctx0026: connect and interact with others express themselves and receive information in a continuous digital reflection of the real offline world. In OSNs people typically showcase real self 40
ctx0027: and leave traces in online behavior reflect real-world personality 24 . traces expose a holistic image of oneself including both personal characteristics personality traits and characteristics that portray
ctx0028: behavior in relation to others relational traits . The term personality refers to characteristic combinations or patterns of behaviors cognitions and emotional reactions that evolve from biological and
ctx0029: environmental factors and form relatively consistent individual differences 13 . The Big Five BF or Five Factor model 29 is one of the most distinctive personality theories that
ctx0030: constitutes five main traits of human personality representing individual differences in cognition emotion and behavior: Openness to Experience Conscientiousness Extraversion Agreeableness and Neuroticism. On the other hand relational
ctx0031: traits have also been linked with consistencies in social behavior and interaction patterns with attachment theory 7 as the most emblematic theoretical framework in that respect 31 43
ctx0032: capturing how individuals experience close relationships to and interactions with others. Personality traits have been studied in the context of OSNs and the web overall as findings show
ctx0033: that they are strongly linked to OSN use 57 online friendships 60 and online reviews 52 . Moreover certain prediction models have been proposed 37 64 to extract
ctx0034: users psychological background from online behavioral residue and map it to personality characteristics. However relational traits such as attachment orientations AO have been overlooked in online environments even
ctx0035: though user activity in OSNs heavily relates to social behavior characteristics. This makes the study of a relational profile critical from an application point of view and provides
ctx0036: rich information individuals social profile. The present research aims to address this limitation in OSN research by studying and predicting both relational traits and personality traits of users.
ctx0037: The importance of relational facets of personality for explaining social interaction cannot be overstated. that online social media engagement resembles actual social interactions in many respects 15 30
ctx0038: the need to study how different personality facets are reflected in online expression is particularly compelling. Attachment orientations a key individual difference of relational orientation can be derived
ctx0039: on the basis of traces found in micro-blogs. Attachment orientations capture one s notion of the self in relation to others and interpersonal relationships with attachment theory being
ctx0040: one of the key personality theoretical frames to actual social behavior 31 . Considering both traditional personality Big Five traits and relational traits is important for 1 providing
ctx0041: holistic profiling of OSN users humans have an integrated profile in self and social aspects interrelate and affect each other and joint profiling can be essential for understanding
ctx0042: the overall human presence on OSNs 2 uncovering more traits of people s psychological and social world has been identified as a direction in OSN research currently focuses
ctx0043: on the personality traits that help to better analyze and predict online user behavior 66 e.g. with applications on customer segmentation 46 or digital advertisement environments 17 and
ctx0044: 3 shedding light on social interaction phenomena taking place in OSNs is of great socioeconomic importance e.g. community formation 32 decision making 42 or information diffusion 12 .
ctx0045: To this end the present article proposes a novel data-driven approach to predict a holistic psychological profile of OSN users capturing both personality and relational traits.1 Building on
ctx0046: insights stemming from psychology theory our approach applies data mining on OSN textual and non-textual data carefully selects different sets of features for predicting different types of traits
ctx0047: and exploits the inherent correlations in psychological traits to efficiently predict a complete image of OSN users psychological profile. The proposed approach is applied on the Twitter micro-blogging
ctx0048: service stands as a live dynamic and open OSN platform on people intensively interact and is largely driven by people s spontaneous reactions and emotions expressing themselves personality
ctx0049: facet and interacting with others relational facet at the same time. Specifically our contributions in detail are as follows: Data mining and feature engineering for psychology traces in
ctx0050: OSN. Motivated by psychology theory on personality suggesting that traits are reflected in different types of online behavior and actions we identify a large set of features that
ctx0051: capture language behavioral and emotional expressions of users in OSNs. The proposed feature engineering methodology accounts for a larger set of features than considered in previous works thus
ctx0052: allowing to target more generic psychological profiling. To apply and test our methodology we collected a labeled dataset: through a crowdsourcing platform we recruited 243 individuals who consented
ctx0053: to provide information psychology profiles. Subsequently we compiled a ground-truth dataset labeled with psychology profiles. We used the Twitter API to collect 350 000 tweets from the Twitter
ctx0054: accounts of recruited participants and applied the proposed feature engineering methodology. Holistic psychological profiling. We propose a novel machine learning ML methodology to predict users holistic psychological profile
ctx0055: including both Big Five personality and relational traits. The novelty of the proposed methodology is that it 1 uses a large set of the collected psychological-related features 2
ctx0056: carefully selects the subsets of them with the strongest predictive power for each trait and 3 exploits correlations between personality and relational i.e. social behavior traits to enhance
ctx0057: individual trait predictions. In this way our approach not predicts social facets of a psychology profile is not captured by existing personality prediction models along with personality facets
ctx0058: but also leverages the different traits for more accurate holistic profile prediction. New insights and improvement of prediction accuracy. Evaluating our methodology reveals interesting insights for the prediction
ctx0059: of psychology traits from OSN traces: 1 different sets of features performs better in predicting different psychological traits 2 relational traits can be predicted as efficiently as personality
ctx0060: traits and 3 holistic personality prediction outperforms individual trait predicting models. We believe that our findings can pave the ground for future experimentation and studies in psychology profiling
ctx0061: in OSNs. Moreover the accuracy achieved by our approach across all traits is higher than current state-of-the-art approaches currently are limited to Big Five personality traits instead of
ctx0062: relational traits. For example applying the approach of 12 to our data provides a root mean squared error RMSE of 0.284 while our prediction model achieves a 29
ctx0063: improvement for personality traits RMSE 0.203 and has 32 better average performance when accounting for all traits 0.192 RMSE this improvement comes as a result of both a
ctx0064: psychology-driven feature engineering methodology and a holistic profiling approach. Psychological profiling in the wild. We demonstrate the applicability of the proposed psychological profiling methodology through a use case.
ctx0065: We identify a set of Twitter users who seem to be accepted as opinion leaders on social media i.e. have a large following . We apply our methodology
ctx0066: to predict psychological profiles and analyze results. We find that the distributions of traits significantly deviates from regular users defined as users included in our ground-truth dataset and
ctx0067: that the set of leaders can be clearly separated by psychological profiles. findings highlight the usefulness of our approach in the characterization of the personalities for different groups
ctx0068: of OSN users e.g. such a group psychological profile be used to recommend skills/activities/jobs to users based on profile similarity and classification of users based on profiles. In
ctx0069: this section we provide an overview of related psychological literature discuss related work and highlight several open issues of existing methods. We also highlight the contribution of the
ctx0070: present work. Section 3 details the collected dataset and the data mining and feature engineering methodology and Section 4 presents the design and evaluation of the proposed machine

ctx0082: Audiences Many managers believe that they will never have to deal with the press. Often they regard it with hostility. Most think press relations are entirely the domain
ctx0083: of company s or agency s public relations department. But in fact senior executives say they spend more time on communications than on other tasks and a significant
ctx0084: component of that time is devoted to press and public relations. Junior managers need to be highly sensitive to press relations for the following reasons: Often free press
ctx0085: can be the best way to acquaint the public with your product or service. To cite one example the amount Microsoft spent on advertising Windows 95 was dwarfed
ctx0086: by the value of the free publicity it from international news coverage. Your particular area of expertise may unexpectedly become something your organization needs to promote or Line
ctx0087: workers at auto companies have been drafted to extol quality improvements in advertisements accountants may be called to the CEO s office for briefings on a potentially embarrassing
ctx0088: news report or an upcoming press conference. Public relations considerations need to be addressed at the beginning not the end of a planning process. Business history is replete
ctx0089: with examples of companies that invested vast sums to develop products ideas or services that couldn t be sold because of public resistance to the concept the configuration
ctx0090: or the public image of the company. General Motors Tacos for example be the best in the world and still not jump off the shelves. Junior managers become
ctx0091: senior managers who will eventually have to deal with the press directly. As both marketers and corporate citizens organizations have to themselves to the public constantly through advertising
ctx0092: press releases and press conferences. Junior managers who understand this aspect of work are likely to become senior managers faster. 1. A successful manager understands how the press
ctx0093: works. Successful managers tend to the press in general and how organization is playing in particular. Members of the press tend to trust companies and individuals with a
ctx0094: track record of accuracy and accessibility. To cite two examples both Johnson Johnson and Perrier survived charges of contaminated products because they had a record of reliability and
ctx0095: accessibility and addressed the problems immediately. In both cases and many others stonewalling have been disastrous to the company s image of wholesomeness and purity. Most press stories
ctx0096: last a few days but they can leave an indelible impression in the public s mind. Many managers tend to believe they can snow the press with greater
ctx0097: expertise but this strategy rarely works. Most reporters are hard-working professionals who will carefully check out an expert assertion or who know someone who can. 2. A successful
ctx0098: manager understands what the press needs. What the press needs is a story and bad news generally sells better than good news. Companies and individuals are most likely
ctx0099: to have to deal with the press when something has gone wrong. This suggests a couple of lessons. When you have good stories give them to the press
ctx0100: to establish a record of credibility many media outlets will print or broadcast a press release from a reliable source more or less verbatim. Consider how private decisions
ctx0101: may look if they become public. If something has gone wrong take the initiative in announcing it explaining it and telling the world how it s going to
ctx0102: be corrected. 3. A successful manager understands press jargon. Reputable reporters will stick to verbal agreements on how information you provide them is to be used. How you
ctx0103: will be quoted depends on the ground rules you establish at the beginning of an interview. Deep background means the reporter can reflect the information in her story
ctx0104: without possible attribution. Background means that you can be referenced as a reliable source. Any other comment however apparently casual or social can be quoted directly and attributed.
ctx0105: 4. A successful manager be able to generate an attention-grabbing accurate and well-constructed press release. While many managers may not be regularly mailing out press releases themselves most
ctx0106: will be contributing to them and need to understand how they work. A good press release is extremely formulaic and follows the structure of a good news story:
ctx0107: a. The first paragraph states the main point clearly and emphasizes its newsworthiness. For example: Acme Corporation announced today that it is releasing the best tire ever available
ctx0108: on the world market. b. The second paragraph provides a quote from a reputable source: Acme President Rudy Roadrunner said Not does this tire surpass all our competitors
ctx0109: in endurance quality and safety it s also available at a lower price. c. The third paragraph provides evidence that the claims made so far are true: In
ctx0110: repeated tests against our competitors . . . d. The remaining paragraphs provide background information on the product the company and Rudy Roadrunner and they demonstrate a track
ctx0111: record of credibility. They may also include testimonials available from respected independent sources. Obviously the formula of an effective press release will vary depending on the nature of
ctx0112: the news to be announced. But the pyramid structure suggested by this example always applies: Move from the most important and specific to the least important and most
ctx0113: general information. Busy editors often run a press release more or less verbatim and just cut it off when they run out of space. The easier you make
ctx0114: jobs the more likely they are to cover your story. Once you ve written or contributed to a press release decide who s most likely to run it.

ctx0117: This includes knowing how to provide effective interviews and understanding when and how to hold a press conference. Certain rules apply to both: Applications a. Identify your central
